justification: Bap1+/- knockout mice vs WT, chronic asbestos exposure, higher mesothelioma incidence and shorter latency — all criteria met.
Nf2+/- knockout mice treated with asbestos show accelerated mesothelioma versus WT littermates; genetic susceptibility clearly studied.
p53+/- mice given crocidolite asbestos show increased mesothelioma incidence and reduced latency versus WT; all criteria met.
Blm+/- mice injected with asbestos had shorter survival and higher mesothelioma incidence versus controls; susceptibility studied.
NLRP3-deficient mice vs WT given asbestos, mesothelioma incidence and survival measured; genetic modification effect on susceptibility studied.
NF2+/- and NF2+/-CR-/- mice exposed to asbestos, survival compared between genotypes; biomarker-focused but genotype susceptibility comparison present.
Nf2+/-;Cdkn2a+/- genotype is fixed model background; study compares chemopreventive drugs versus vehicle, not the genetic modification's effect on susceptibility.
MexTAg transgenic vs WT asbestos mesothelioma compared, but outcome is gene expression rather than incidence/latency; borderline, retained conservatively.
MexTAg genotype fixed across arms; study tests dietary antioxidants versus diet, not the genetic modification's effect on mesothelioma susceptibility.
p53+/- mice with crocidolite asbestos positive control develop mesothelioma; nanotube-focused with no genotype comparison, but retained conservatively as borderline.
Ribozyme therapy study in human mesothelioma cell line VAMT-1; no in-vivo mouse genetic susceptibility experiment or asbestos exposure administered.
No abstract; title on asbestos inhalation carcinogenesis is topic-adjacent, cannot confidently rule out; retained per no-abstract conservative rule.
Biochemical study of scavenger receptor binding to asbestos in transfected CHO cells; no in-vivo mouse model or mesothelioma outcome.
Study conducted in rats with chrysotile and radon; no mesotheliomas occurred and no genetic modification involved.
Primary calretinin-knockout mouse mesothelial cells studied in vitro for proliferation and migration; no asbestos exposure or mesothelioma outcome in vivo.
Wild-type C57BL/6 mice and rats given carbon nanotubes/asbestos; immunosuppressive cell study with no defined genetic modification of susceptibility.
